title: lathyrus atropatanus
intro: Lathyrus atropatanus, also known as the Azeri grass pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Azerbaijan. The plant contains a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid) that has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Lathyrus atropatanus has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although it is essential to consume it in moderation and under medical supervision due to its toxicity.
